mnemonics: UML FRAMES Requirements Models: Use Cases, Models, Lists, Flow, Attributes, Relationships, Elements, Scenarios
keyPoints: Requirements modeling describes what a system should do, not how it does it.
It uses visual models like use case diagrams, data flow diagrams, and class diagrams to represent system functionality and structure.
The primary goal is to clarify and communicate requirements to stakeholders and developers.
commonMistakes: Focusing on implementation details instead of the 'what' and 'why' of the requirements.
Creating overly complex models that are difficult for stakeholders to understand and validate.
Failing to update the requirements model as the project evolves, leading to outdated specifications.
